articles.title: Australia has had housing crises before: what can we learn from them? | Anne O’Brien
articles.description: When governments make access to housing more equitable, it’s because they recognise its social and economic value The federal government’s first major tax reform in nearly 30 years was a significant achievement that suggests we may have turned a corner on the housing crisis.
